$comment: SelectionSpec produced by manually executing docs/ai-pattern-selection-instructions.md against docs/examples/flowspec-sample.json on 2026-07-07. Inventory: registry/dashboard-01.json, registry/sidebar-07.json, registry/login-03.json (all maturity=experimental).

screenTypeResolution.tiebreak: Both profiles matched intents/dataShapes/interactions. Resolved by intent priority order: the step's primary intent 'monitor' is listed first in the dashboard profile and last in report-analytics.

scoreBreakdown.notes: accessibility=7: keyboard/focus inherited from primitives but not audited (contrastChecked=false). dependency=6: all shadcn/npm deps installed, but the pattern's page composition is not wired to a route (overwritten by sidebar-07 during install, see registry item risk.notes). evidence=8: official source, but maturity=experimental.
Navigation shells are structural; the step's content intents (monitor/analyze) do not directly apply. Scored on structural fit. See assumptions: shell-role scoring is a known rubric friction.
Bundled within dashboard-01 (components/site-header.tsx); inherits the screen pattern's score.
Bundled within dashboard-01 (components/section-cards.tsx); inherits the screen pattern's score.
Bundled within dashboard-01 (components/chart-area-interactive.tsx); inherits the screen pattern's score.
Bundled within dashboard-01 (components/data-table.tsx); inherits the screen pattern's score.

assumptions: screenType near-tie (dashboard vs report-analytics) resolved by intent priority order of the canonical profiles; profiles are still reviewed:false drafts, so this tiebreak needs confirmation during profile review.
Block roles bundled inside a shadcn screen pattern are treated as fulfilled by the pattern itself and inherit its score; only app-shell-sidebar had a standalone candidate (sidebar-07).

Shell-role scoring against content intents is a rubric friction: structural roles (app-shell-*) were scored on structural fit rather than intent overlap. Consider a separate rubric for structural roles.
risks: Selection rule violation (recorded, not blocking for experimental inventory): data-driven screen lacks empty/loading/error/permission-denied coverage (dashboard-01 stateCoverage is default+mobile only).
dashboard-01's page composition is not currently wired to any route; implementation layer must re-add the block with --overwrite into a dedicated route before use.
Single-candidate selection: no alternatives existed per role, so scores were not competitive. Inventory expansion needed before scores are meaningful comparatively.

scoreBreakdown.notes: accessibility=7: labeled fields via field primitive, but no error announcement implemented. evidence=8: official source, maturity=experimental.
Bundled within login-03 (components/login-form.tsx); inherits the screen pattern's score.

risks: login-03 is a visual pattern only: no submit handler, validation, or error state. Auth wiring is implementation-layer work.
Single-candidate selection: no alternative auth patterns in inventory.

checklist.everyStepResolvedOrUnresolved: pass — overview resolved to dashboard (tiebreak recorded), signin resolved to auth
checklist.blockRolesFromRequiredBlocks: pass — roles read from composition.requiredBlocks of each selected pattern, not derived from raw facets
checklist.componentsNotSelected: pass — registryDependencies listed as information only; no component selection performed
checklist.rejectedAlternativesAndAssumptionsRecord: pass — no rejected candidates existed (single-candidate inventory, noted as risk); assumptions recorded per step
checklist.requiredStatesInStateCoveragePlan: pass — required ['default'] present in both stateCoveragePlans; broader state gaps recorded as risks
